name: Indonesia Open

venue.name: Istora Senayan
venue.blurb: The cathedral of Indonesian badminton — built in 1961, refreshed in 2018, home to the Indonesia Open since 1982. Few arenas turn the crowd into a 13th player like this one.

stakes: This week in Jakarta is a critical test for India's top shuttlers, offering a chance to solidify Olympic qualification hopes and prove their mettle against the world's elite on a Super 1000 stage.
summary:
India's campaign at the POLYTRON Indonesia Open 2026 is off to a mixed but promising start. Men's Doubles pair Satwiksairaj Rankireddy and Chirag Shetty, seeded third and fresh off a Singapore Open win, are looking to build on their strong form as defending champions. In Men's Singles, Lakshya Sen, seeded eighth, faces a tough R32 opener against Kunlavut Vitidsarn—a rematch of their All England quarter-final last year. Meanwhile, HS Prannoy also drew a challenging first-round opponent in defending champion Anders Antonsen.

On the women's side, PV Sindhu has successfully navigated her opening round, advancing to the Round of 16. This Super 1000 event is a crucial part of her ongoing rebuild, and a deep run here would be a significant boost. Young talent Malvika Bansod faces arguably the toughest possible draw in Akane Yamaguchi, a true test of her mettle. Women's Doubles pair Treesa Jolly and Gayatri Gopichand enter the tournament in career-best form, hoping to make a mark against the Korean pair of Kim/Kong. India's mixed doubles entry, Dhruv Kapila and Tanisha Crasto, also face a monumental task, drawn against the world #1 pair Zheng/Huang.
